documentation.description:
This package provides the ``memmove`` function as defined by the C
standard. ``memmove`` calls are directly generated by the compiler to
perform record or array copies when source and targets cannot overlap.

subprograms.name: memmove
subprograms.code:
function memmove
  (Dest : Address; Src : Address; N : Memory_Types.size_t) return Address

documentation.description:
Copies ``N`` storage units from area starting at ``Src`` to area
starting at ``Dest`` without any check for buffer overflow. The
difference between this *memmove* and *memcpy* is that with *memmove*,
the storage areas may overlap (forwards or backwards) and the result is
correct (i.e. it is as if ``Src`` is first moved to a temporary area,
and then this area is copied to ``Dst`` in a separate step).

If the length ``N`` is 0, this procedure returns immediately.

If the destination ``Dest`` overlaps the range ``Src`` to ``Src`` +
``N``, this routine performs a backward copy, and iterates over the
bytes by starting from the last one.

Otherwise, this routine performs a forward copy. If both ``Dest`` and
``Src`` are aligned on a word, copy word per word. Then copy the
remaining data byte per byte.
